data.content:
I can see the problem. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. MSBuild globs are evaluated before targets run, so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

The fix is to add the generated file to `Compile` (and `FileWrites` for proper clean behavior) within the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file `obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`, but it never adds it to the `Compile` item group. The file is generated before `CoreCompile`, but since globs are evaluated at evaluation time (before any targets run), the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it with `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

This is the classic "including generated files" issue - the target needs to explicitly add the file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` item groups after generating it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. Glob-based `<Compile>` items are evaluated at project load time — before any targets execute — so dynamically generated files are invisible unless explicitly added.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to:
1. `<Compile Include="...">` — includes the generated file in compilation
2. `<FileWrites Include="...">` — registers it so `dotnet clean` removes it
